{{Quote|Benno don't ''like'' thinking!|Frazznik|<ref name="kotor3" />}}
'''Benno''' was a leader in the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]] [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]' [[Lower City]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. In approximately [[3964 BBY]], Benno commanded a crew of several gang members, including the [[Drovian]] [[Wargo]] and the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Frazznik]], and was well-known as an impatient and gruff individual not given to deep thought.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Benno hates to wait. Cashing a bounty from the constable's gonna mean bureaucracy, authorizations, delay—and they may make him come up-city to testify. Just picture ''Benno, up-city!''<nowiki></nowiki>|Marn Hierogryph|<ref name="kotor3" />}}
Benno was a member of the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]] [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]' [[Lower City]] in the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. By around [[3964 BBY]], Benno had become a leader within the Vulkars, commanding a crew that included the [[Drovian]] [[Wargo]] and the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Frazznik]]. Around that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Benno's crew captured two wanted fugitives—[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]], accused of [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] four of his classmates in Taris' [[Jedi Tower]], and an accomplice, local [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] hood [[Marn Hierogryph]]. However, Hierogryph, who was familiar with Benno's impatience and his dislike for [[Upper City]] ways, convinced Wargo and Frazznik to let them go rather than turn them over to Benno so he could claim the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on their heads.<ref name="kotor3" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''Hrrmm''. Benno don't like to wear city clothes.|Frazznik|<ref name="kotor3" />}}
A gruff and unintellectual individual, Benno was regarded by his crew as one who did not like to think. Benno was impatient and disliked Upper City bureaucracy and delays, along with having to wear Upper City [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]]. Benno inspired a measure of respect and fear within the members of his crew, who would go to great lengths so as not to displease him.<ref name="kotor3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Benno was mentioned in [[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]]<ref name="kotor3" /> and released on [[March 29]], [[2006]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-878/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-3|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #3}}</ref>
